summary.intent: Fix a dead-end UX bug where remote users on a fresh Moltis deployment were redirected to a static '/setup-required' page instead of the onboarding wizard, leaving them unable to complete first-time auth setup.
summary.outcome: Successfully redirected remote SetupRequired traffic to '/onboarding', rewrote the setup-required HTML to explain the setup code flow, updated all related tests and assertions, committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: The onboarding AuthStep (crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js) already supported remote setup-code entry — no JS changes were needed.
should_redirect_from_onboarding in crates/web/src/templates.rs already returns false when auth_setup_pending=true, preventing redirect loops during remote setup.

The original /setup-required redirect (commit fd01fb94, PR #356) was added to fix both a redirect loop and a security concern; the loop was already fixed separately and the security is covered by the setup code requirement.
Project policy: never commit without explicit user request — changes were left unstaged until user said 'commit push and create a PR'.
Beads issue tracking is used: issues are created and closed as part of the implementation workflow.

code.finding: auth_gate AuthResult::SetupRequired branch for remote non-onboarding paths — changed redirect target from /setup-required to /onboarding
setup_required_handler guard redirects to /login when setup is complete — kept intact for stale bookmark handling
setup_required_template_renders_html test previously asserted presence of 'moltis auth reset-password' string — updated to assert new heading, 'setup code' text, and href='/onboarding'
Setup code validation happens server-side here — remote attacker without stdout access cannot complete /api/auth/setup even if they reach /onboarding
In-memory setup code is generated here on server start — only visible in stdout

workflow: Background bash tasks (run_in_background) were used for long-running cargo builds; results read via TaskOutput when notifications arrived.
Compilation was verified before running tests to catch pre-existing errors vs. regression.
biome check --write was run on modified JS files as part of the lint step.
cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all -- --check was used for Rust formatting verification per CLAUDE.md.

friction: Hit rate limit mid-session ('You've hit your limit · resets 8pm (Europe/Lisbon)') — required user to issue 'proceed' twice to continue.
Template test had a compile error that needed investigation to determine if it was pre-existing or caused by the changes.
Feature flags (vault, ssh) gating certain crates required running tests with specific --features flags to get a clean compile.
page-settings.js had references to 'setup-required page' that needed investigation to confirm they didn't require updates.
open_items: E2E Playwright test (crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/onboarding-auth.spec.js) was updated but not actually run in CI during this session — full Playwright run against a live instance still needed.
Manual repro against a real docker-compose deployment with MOLTIS_NO_TLS=true was not performed — only automated tests verified.
The moltis auth reset-password CLI path is now documented only as a footnote in setup-required.html; a link to docs was referenced but actual docs page may need updating.
